📄

Recipe Curator — Persona Source (Markdown)

📦 v1.4.1 📏 276 lines 🎯 Standalone persona 

  






  











  
AI Insights

  
Persona Crafting & Multi-Agent Workflow

  

  
v1.1.3 · 2026-06-15

  
Sebastian Mordziol

  

    Welcome everyone. Today I'll share how we brought engineering discipline to AI agent instructions —
    and what happens when you give AI agents structure, persistent memory, and defined roles.
  






  
What We'll Cover

  

    
Part 1 — Persona Philosophy

       What agent personas are and how to craft them effectively.

    
Part 2 — The Build System

       Write once, deploy to every AI platform.

    
Part 3 — The Ledger & Workflow

       How 9 agents coordinate through a shared notebook.

    
Demo — The Ledger GUI

       An overview of ledger features through the GUI.

  

  

    Three parts plus a short live demo at the end. The focus is on the "why" and the philosophy behind
    persona curation — the technical parts are kept visual and accessible.
  






  
Part 1

  
Persona Philosophy

  
The foundation of everything.






  
Discovering personas

  
What is an Agent Persona?

  
A structured instruction document that tells an AI

     who it is, what it does, and how it works.

  

  
More than a prompt — an operating manual.

  

  

    Our running example: the Recipe Curator
  

    

    "Imagine an AI that's genuinely good at helping you cook your way—

    and that knows your kitchen inside and out."
  

  

    We'll use the Recipe Curator throughout — it's a real production persona from our standalone suite.
    Non-technical, universally relatable. Everyone understands cooking.
  






  
Discovering personas

  
The Persona Difference

  

    Same prompt: "Please give me a pasta carbonara recipe." — Gemini Web
  

  

    

      
Without persona

      

        Authentic Roman Pasta Carbonara


        Textbook recipe. Guanciale, eggs, Pecorino.

        Generic quantities, no personalization.

        Imperial + metric mix.

        No nutrition data, no variations.
      

    

    

      
With Recipe Curator (Gem)

      

        Rigatoni alla Carbonara di Zucchine

        Roman (Lazio region)


        Uses garden herbs by name.

        References household equipment.

        Metric only. Color groups listed.

        Nutrition table + Tinkerer's Notes + Shopping List.
      

    

  

  

    Both results from Gemini Web, same prompt. The bare AI gives the most obvious answer from page 1 of
    a search engine. The persona-driven AI creates something novel — respects the no-fish policy (uses
    canned mackerel, not fresh), names the Fissler pan, pulls savory from the garden. The persona IS
    the difference.
  






  
The Craft of Persona Curation

  
Natural Language Programming

  
Writing personas is programming — without code.

  

    
The Recipe Curator agent's Workflow section:

    
    
      
  1. Check In — ask what they've cooked recently
    2. 
      
  2. Understand the Request — quick lookup or weekly plan?
    3. 
      
  3. Survey Options — generate 3+ candidates across traditions
    4. 
      
  4. Adapt & Compose — tailor to household
    5. 
      
  5. Format Output — use the standard template
    6. 
      
  6. Verify Targets — check nutrition & color coverage
    7. 
      
  7. Handoff — end with status marker
    8. 
    

  

  

  
Same logical structure as a processing pipeline. You define inputs, outputs, control flow, and error handling.

  

    This is the key insight for the technical audience: persona curation IS programming. For the non-technical
    audience: it's a recipe for how the AI should think. You debug personas the same way you debug code —
    observe the output, trace the logic, adjust the instructions.
  






  
The Craft of Persona Curation

  
Models speak Markdown

  
Natural language, but with structure.

  

    
Human-readable — authors and reviewers need no special tools

    
AI-native — models parse Markdown natively

    
Semantically structured — headings create structure

    
Formatting as a tool — bold text for emphasis

    
Version-controllable — Git diffs show exactly what changed

    
Platform-portable — every AI tool accepts plain text

  

  

    📄 Click to view the full persona source  →
  

  

    The entire Recipe Curator persona is a single Markdown file.
  

  

    This is the actual Recipe Curator persona — 276 lines of Markdown. No JSON, no YAML config, no
    proprietary format. The headings create semantic structure that the AI interprets naturally.
    Markdown is the lingua franca of AI instructions. Click the document icon in the top right at
    any time to see the full source.
  






  
The Craft of Persona Curation

  
Persona Document Structure

  
Mission first, workflow last. The AI reads top-to-bottom — frontload what matters.

  

    
1 Mission — Identity anchor + core purpose

    
2 Operating Philosophy — Values & principles (rainbow plate, seasonal…)

    
3 Inputs — What the user can provide

    
4 Outputs — Templates with exact format (recipe, meal plan)

    
5 Reference Data — Kitchen equipment, Rainbow Eating table

    
6 Strict Constraints — Nutrition targets, no tuna, metric only

    
7 Workflow — Step-by-step processing pipeline (anchored last)

  

  

  
276 lines — not a one-liner.

  

    The structure matters. Mission comes first because it's the most important context for the AI. The
    workflow comes last because it anchors the behavior — the AI has already absorbed the context and
    constraints by the time it reaches the step-by-step instructions.
  






  
The Craft of Persona Curation

  
The Identity Anchor

  

    Establishes identity firmly over longer sessions.
  

  

    

      
Unreliable

      
"You are a chef."

    

    

      
Stronger

      

        "**Identity: Private Chef & Culinary Consultant.**

        Curate, adapt, and compose recipes tailored to a home kitchen…"
      

    

  

  

    ›
    Mirrors how system prompts work internally.
  

  
💡 TIP: Using "Please" in instructions subtly shifts the model into a positive, cooperative bias.

  

    The AI doesn't need to BE something — it needs to DO something. The identity anchor defines the
    function, not the character. This is more stable over long conversations. And a small practical tip:
    using "please" in your instructions genuinely affects the model's tone and cooperativeness.
  






  
The Craft of Persona Curation

  
Why English Works Best

  
Language models store concepts, not words.

  
But they're trained predominantly on English text.

  

  

    Prompts in English → more predictable results

    even when the output is in another language.
  

  

  

    From the Recipe Curator:

    "Match the User's Language — respond in the language the user writes in."


    The persona is in English. The output adapts to the user.
  

  

    Practical tip for this multilingual audience. Write your persona in English, let the AI handle
    the output language. The model can juggle languages perfectly well — but the instruction layer
    is most reliable in English because that's where the training data is densest.
  






  
What Makes a Persona Powerful

  
Domain Knowledge

  
Context the AI can't guess — so you give it.

  

    

      
EquipmentCapabilities

      
        
Cast-iron wok (large)High-heat stir-fry, deep-frying, smoking

        
Römertopf (clay roaster)Slow-roasting with steam, no-fat cooking

        
Krampouz crêpe machine (44 cm)Thin crêpes, galettes de sarrasin

        
WMF Pressure cookerFast stocks, braises, legumes, steaming

        
Fissler deep sauté panOne-pot dishes, braises, sauces with volume

        
Gas planchaOutdoor flat-top grilling, high-heat searing

        
Small flour millFresh-milled flour from whole grains

      
    

  

  

  
A persona isn't just rules — it's knowledge.

  

    This is the table from the actual persona. The AI now knows what's in the kitchen — so it
    suggests recipes that USE this equipment. The Smoked Mackerel Carbonara referenced the Fissler
    pan and the gas stove by name. If you don't have a piece of equipment, the persona tells the AI
    to adapt the technique instead.
  






  
What Makes a Persona Powerful

  
Values & Standards

  
A recipe quality standard the AI enforces automatically.

  

    

      
Color GroupKey PhytonutrientsExamples

      
        
RedLycopene, ellagic acidTomatoes, red peppers, strawberries

        
Orange & YellowBeta-carotene, hesperidinCarrots, sweet potatoes, mango

        
GreenLutein, sulforaphaneSpinach, broccoli, zucchini

        
Blue & PurpleAnthocyanins, resveratrolEggplant, blueberries, figs

        
Dark Red / MagentaBetalainsBeets, rhubarb, ruby chard

        
White / Tan / BrownAllicin, quercetinGarlic, mushrooms, cauliflower

      
    

  

  

  

    Equipment = situational awareness  | 
    Rainbow Eating = quality standards

    Together: the AI makes judgment calls, not just follows commands.
  

  

    Per-meal target: 2-3 color groups. Per-week: all 6 must appear. The AI checks this automatically
    on every recipe and flags gaps. This is the key takeaway: equipment gives situational awareness,
    rainbow eating gives quality standards. Together, they let the AI make judgment calls. This is the
    difference between a prompt and a persona.
  






  
Beyond a single persona

  
Meet the Team

  
19 standalone personas — each a specialist you can invoke on demand.

  

    

      
📋

      
Planner

      
Actionable implementation plans from feature requests

    

    

      
💻

      
Developer

      
Plan execution with code insights and synthesis

    

    

      
🔍

      
Researcher

      
Deep-dive analysis of complex problems

    

    

      
🧪

      
Unit Test Auditor

      
Coverage gaps, weak assertions, missing edge cases

    

    

      
📝

      
Changelog Curator

      
Git history to scannable changelogs in house style

    

    

      
🎭

      
Persona Curator

      
Create and audit personas with the design guide

    

    

      
📦

      
Git Committer

      
Structured commits with plan traceability

    

    

      
🍳

      
Recipe Curator

      
Meal planning & recipe curation with nutritional standards

    

  

  
+ 11 more: Documentation, Manifest Curator, Readme Curator…

  

    This is just a selection. Each persona is a full specialist with its own mission, constraints,
    workflow, and quality standards. They range from coding agents to documentation curators to
    personal-use personas like the Recipe Curator. The key point: the same persona-crafting discipline
    applies whether you're building a code review agent or a meal planner.
  






  
Part 2

  
The Build System

  
Write once, deploy everywhere.






  
Catering to multiple platforms

  
Where Can Personas Be Used?

  

  

    
● VS Code, Cursor, Claude Code

    
Fully supported (global + project)

    
● Gemini Web

    
With Gems

    
● Claude Web & Desktop

    
With Project instructions

    
● Copilot Web

    
Not yet — manual copy & paste

    
● ChatGPT Web

    
Project instructions (8k limit) or "GPTs" (paywall)

    
● Grok Web

    
With Custom Agents (4 max, paywall)

  

  

    The key point: personas aren't tied to one tool. You can use them almost anywhere. But each platform
    has its own format for how the instructions are loaded — which is exactly the problem the build
    system solves.
  






  
Catering to multiple platforms

  
The Scaling Problem

  
One persona. Many targets.

  
Each with its own metadata format.

  

  

    VS Code needs YAML frontmatter.

    Claude Code needs a different header.

    Deep Agents need plain Markdown.
  

  

  

    The persona builder: a templating system

    to write once, deploy everywhere.
  

  

    Maintaining copies by hand doesn't scale. One change to the persona means updating 3 files in 3
    different formats. The persona builder solves this with a template engine.
  






  
Catering to multiple platforms

  
Build Pipeline

  

  

    

      Sources

      YAML metadata
+ Markdown templates
+ Shared partials
    

    
›

    

      Build Engine

      Resolve variables
Apply conditionals
Inject partials
    

    
›

    

      Targets

      VS Code .agent.md
Claude Code .md
Deep Agents .md
    

  

  

  

    Shared partials handle cross-cutting concerns:

    operational protocols, output formats, incident logging.
  

  

    Single source of truth. One change propagates to all targets. Shared partials mean that if you
    change how all agents should log incidents, you change one file and rebuild. No code on screen
    intentionally — this is the conceptual flow.
  






  
Catering to multiple platforms

  
One Source, Multiple Outputs

  
Target conditionals adapt to each platform automatically.

  

    {{#if target_vscode}}

      Invoke `runSubagent` with agentName: "WP Decomposer"

    {{else if target_claude_code}}

      Use the `Task` tool with agent: "WP Decomposer"

    {{else if target_deep_agents}}

      Use the `task` tool with subagent_type: "wp-decomposer"

    {{/if}}
  

  

    From the Project Manager persona — each platform has a different tool API

    for dispatching sub-agents. The template handles it.
  

  

    Variables {{agent_name}}
    |
    Partials {{> shared-partial}}
    |
    Conditionals {{#if}}
  

  

    This is real syntax from the Project Manager persona. VS Code uses runSubagent, Claude Code uses
    Task, Deep Agents uses task with a slug. One source file, three correct outputs. The template
    language has just three constructs: variables for values, partials for shared content blocks,
    and conditionals for platform differences. No programming required.
  






  
Part 3

  
The Ledger & Workflow

  
A team of 9 interconnected AI specialists.






  
Building a virtuous chain

  
The Agentic Workflow

  
Granular precision with well-defined personas.

  

    

      
👤 Human

      

        
1

        
Planner

        
Scope & plan

      

    

    
›

    

      
👤 Human

      

        
2

        
PM

        
Decompose & schedule

      

      

        Decomposer
        Sequencer
        Configurator
        Bootstrapper
      

    

    
›

    

      

        
3

        
Developer

        
Implement

      

      
›

      

        
4

        
QA

        
Test & verify

      

      
›

      

        
5

        
Security

        
Audit

      

      
›

      

        
6

        
Reviewer

        
Code review

      

      
›

      

        
7

        
Release

        
Ship it

      

      
›

      

        
8

        
Docs

        
Document

      

      
↻ repeat per work package

    

    
›

    

      
👤 Human

      

        
9

        
Synthesis

        
Wrap up & learn

      

      

        Knowledge Archiver
      

    

  

  

    The full development lifecycle in one chain — and beyond:

    extracting lessons so the next project starts smarter.
  

  

    Nine specialized agents, each with a defined role. The 👤 markers show where a human initiates
    the agent — Planner, PM, and Synthesis are human-driven stages. The middle stages (3–8) run
    per work package. The PM and Synthesis have subagents shown below their cards (yellow borders).
    Each agent has its own persona built by the same build system we just discussed.
  






  
Building a virtuous chain

  
Dynamic Pipelines

  
Not every task needs all 9 stages. The PM agent decides on the stage sequencing.

  
  

  

    WP-006: docs only. WP-001: rework badges — the pipeline caught issues and looped back.
  

  

    This is a screenshot from the actual ledger GUI. Notice WP-006 has only DOC. WP-003 and 004 include
    the security audit stage. WP-001 has red rework badges on DEV and QA — meaning those stages found
    issues, sent the work back, and the developer fixed them. The pipeline is adaptive.
  






  
Interconnecting multiple agents

  
The Coordination Problem

  
Nine agents need to share state.

  
AI has no long-term memory between sessions.

  
Memory concepts are tool-specific (e.g. Claude).

  

  

    What if we gave them a shared notebook?
  

  

    This is the core problem the ledger solves. Each agent runs in its own session — when it ends,
    the context is gone. The ledger is the persistent state that bridges the gap.
  






  
Interconnecting multiple agents

  
The Project Ledger

  
A platform-independent, persistent MCP notebook that workflow agents read from and write to.

  

    

      
📋 Shared Memory

      
The state that makes coordination possible. Lives on disk, survives across sessions.

    

    

      
🔀 Handoff Authority

      
Each agent asks "what should I do next?" — the ledger routes them based on project state, not human intervention.

    

    

      
👁️ Observation Log

      
Every agent can add observations. The ledger accumulates collective intelligence.

    

  

  

    Three roles in one. The observation log is particularly powerful — problems surface organically
    as each specialist examines the work through their lens. No single agent needs to see everything;
    the ledger accumulates collective intelligence. And it's just JSON files on disk — completely
    tool-agnostic.
  






  
Interconnecting multiple agents

  
Knowledge That Persists

  
Elevate curated project insights into institutional memory.

  

    

      
🌍 Global Knowledge

      
Curated cross-repository insights: architectural patterns, tooling decisions, workflow lessons.

Survives across all projects.

    

    

      
📦 Repository Knowledge

      
Codebase-specific facts: conventions, gotchas, module dependencies.

Scoped to one repo, always available.

    

  

  

  

    Synthesis extracts insights → future Planners & PMs query the store before decisions.

    Each project also carries a project strategy — high-level vision that aligns agent work.
  

  

    The Knowledge Archiver (subagent of Synthesis) extracts insights at project end. Future projects
    start smarter. Two scopes: global knowledge applies everywhere, repository knowledge is codebase-specific.
    The project strategy is a separate concept — a high-level vision and timeline context.
  






  
Interconnecting multiple agents

  
The Orchestrator

  
Same workflow, headless in the command line.

  

    

      
Semi-Interactive (IDE)

      

        Agents hand off automatically through the ledger in VS Code.


        Human involved at key stages
(Planner, PM, Synthesis).


        
            Deterministic & observable.

            Live GUI tracking.
        
      

    

    

      
Headless (Orchestrator)

      

        One command launches the full workflow.


        LangGraph routes agents automatically.

        Same MCP server. Same personas.


        
            Ideal for oversightless automation.

            Live GUI tracking.
        
      

    

  

  

  

    Both paths use identical persona prompts and ledger state — the orchestrator is a different driver, not a different system.
  

  

    The IDE path is already deterministic — agents hand off automatically through the ledger. The
    human is involved at Planner, PM, and Synthesis (as shown in the previous slide). The orchestrator
    removes even that — a LangGraph supervisor drives the entire chain from a single CLI command.
    Same MCP server, same personas, same ledger. Useful for CI, batch processing, or fully hands-off runs.
  






  
Interconnecting multiple agents

  
Battle-Tested

  
Real-world pluridisciplinary usage since January 2026.

  

    

      
420+

      
plans executed

    

  

  

    Personas refined iteratively from real-world usage.

    With the knowledge store, every project's lessons are remembered.
  

  

  

    Experience was drawn from Windows, Linux, and MacOS development.

    Languages include Python, Rust, TypeScript, C#, PHP and more.

    Application targets include web, desktop, and CLI. 
  

  

    Since January 2026, over 420 plans have been run through the workflow. Each run stress-tests the
    personas and surfaces edge cases in agent behavior, handoff logic, and quality standards. The
    current persona set is battle-tested — shaped by real failures and real fixes, not theoretical
    design. This is what gives confidence that the crafting principles work in practice.
  






  
Live Demo

  
The Ledger GUI

  
Let's look at some of the ledger features in the GUI.

  
  

  

    Project overview · Work packages · Pipeline stages · Agent observations · Synthesis report · Knowledge & Strategy
  

  

    Switch to the browser and walk through the GUI. Show the project overview, click into a work
    package, show the pipeline stages with their status badges, scroll through observations left by
    different agents, and show the synthesis report. Keep it conversational. This makes everything
    from Parts 1-3 concrete.
  






  
Key Takeaways

  

  

    
1. Personas bring engineering rigor to AI instructions.

    Not prompts — operating manuals. Versioned, templated, validated.

    
2. Agent personas are useful for everyone.

    Engineer your own personas - for both business and private use.

    
3. A shared ledger turns isolated sessions into a coordinated team.

    Persistent memory + automatic handoffs + collective intelligence.

    
4. This approach works today, with tools you already have.

    VS Code, Claude Code, Gemini, ChatGPT — personas work across all of them.

  

  

    Three things to remember. Personas are engineering artifacts. The ledger creates a team.
    And you can start using this approach today.
  






  
Thank You :)
